component.name: xwpe
component.summary: Programming environment and text editor for UNIX terminals and X11

description.p: xwpe is an integrated programming and debugging environment inspired by the Borland Turbo C and Pascal family. It provides syntax highlighting, integrated compiler and debugger interface, project management, and a function-key driven menu system. Emacs cursor keys are built in.

The 1.6.x series brought xwpe from its 1993 origins to 2026: UTF-8 support, Cairo+Pango rendering with color emoji, 9 compilers (gcc, g++, gfortran, fpc, javac, python3, pdflatex, perl, cobc), 3 debuggers (gdb, jdb, pdb), ncurses mouse with drag support, and interactive program I/O via pty.

screenshot.caption: Editing C code with compiler errors listed in the Messages window
Running on a Wayland desktop through XWayland, with Xft/Cairo rendering

description.p: Smoother window move and resize in both front-ends (X11 and the console via GPM) and a new Cairo+Pango rendering style. Fixes a fatal X11 start crash when the legacy core font is absent and a long-standing debugger memory leak.
Xft rendering, debugger resurrection, UTF-8 everywhere.
Rescue release: new upstream, Debian patches integrated.
